Stipa wulaica is a type of perennial grass native to China. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stipa wulaica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
